tagline_en: Every number that moves an AI supply-chain stock — what it actually measures, where it gets published, which traps eat analysts, and the 43 terms that unlock all ten sector briefs.
tagline_zh: 每一个能撬动 AI 产业链股价的数字——它到底在度量什么、在哪里发布、分析师最常栽进哪些坑，以及解锁全部十个行业篇所需的 43 个术语。

sections.heading_en: Price the output, not the input: perf/TCO, MFU and $/Mtok
sections.heading_zh: 给产出定价，而不是给投入定价：perf/TCO、MFU 与 $/Mtok
sections.body_en:
Most mistakes in AI hardware analysis start the same way: pricing an input (FLOPS, gigawatts, chips) instead of the output (useful tokens per dollar). Three metrics fix that.

TCO — total cost of ownership — is everything a GPU costs over its life: the chip, the servers, networking, power, cooling, and the downtime when it breaks. Perf per TCO divides realized throughput by that all-in cost, and it is the only ratio purchase decisions actually clear on. Example: a GB200 NVL72 rack costs ~1.6x an equivalent H100 fleet on TCO, so it must be ≥1.6x faster to deserve the order — whatever the keynote says.

MFU — model FLOPS utilization — is delivered math divided by the spec-sheet peak. Real training runs at 30-55%, and the number moves with zero hardware change: H100 BF16 MFU went 34%→54% in twelve months on CUDA software alone. So 'peak FLOPS' overstates reality by 2-3x, by a factor that itself keeps changing.

$/Mtok — dollars per million tokens — is where the whole chain cashes out. A GB300 NVL72 rack serving DeepSeek V4 gets to ~$0.156 per million output tokens; every layer of this series exists to push that figure down. And once power, not silicon, is the scarce input, perf/W becomes the tiebreaker: watts are fixed, so whoever converts them to tokens fastest wins the site.
sections.body_zh:
AI 硬件分析里几乎所有错误都始于同一个动作：给投入定价（FLOPS、吉瓦、芯片颗数），而不是给产出定价（每美元能换多少有用 token）。三个指标可以纠正这个习惯。

TCO（总拥有成本）是一颗 GPU 整个生命周期的全部开销：芯片本身、服务器、网络、电力、散热，加上坏掉时的停机损失。perf per TCO 用真实吞吐除以这笔全口径成本——采购决策实际上只认这一个比值。例子：GB200 NVL72 机柜的 TCO 约是等量 H100 的 1.6 倍，所以它必须至少快 1.6 倍才配拿到订单；快、但不到 1.6 倍，就是更差的产品，发布会讲得再热闹也没用。

MFU（模型算力利用率）＝实际跑出的运算量 ÷ 规格表峰值。真实训练只有 30–55%，而且这个数不动硬件也会变：H100 的 BF16 MFU 十二个月里从 34% 升到 54%，纯靠 CUDA 软件。换句话说，「峰值 FLOPS」高估现实 2–3 倍，而这个倍数本身还在变。

$/Mtok（每百万 token 成本）是整条产业链最终的结算单位。一台 GB300 NVL72 跑 DeepSeek V4，可以做到每百万输出 token 约 0.156 美元——本系列的每一层，存在的意义都是把这个数字往下压。而一旦稀缺的投入从硅变成电，perf/W 就成了决胜项：瓦数是死的，谁能把瓦最快变成 token，谁就赢下那块场地。

sections.heading_en: Supply-side dials: HBM content, CoWoS, WFE, capex and cost per wafer
sections.heading_zh: 供给侧仪表盘：HBM 含量、CoWoS、WFE、资本开支与晶圆成本
sections.body_en:
The supply chain publishes its own gauges, and each prices a different layer.

HBM content per GPU — how many gigabytes of stacked memory ride on each accelerator — is the memory sector's demand multiplier. HBM is already ~50%+ of an H100's manufacturing cost and ~60%+ on Blackwell: when Nvidia grows, SK Hynix's revenue line is mechanically dragged along.

CoWoS capacity is the shipment forecaster. Every big AI GPU must pass through TSMC's advanced-packaging step, so its monthly wafer capacity — ~75,000 wafers/month in 2025, roughly double 2024 — caps unit shipments regardless of 'demand'. When someone forecasts GPU units, check the CoWoS arithmetic first.

WFE (wafer-fab equipment) spending is the tool layer's revenue pool, and capex intensity — capex over revenue — shows how hard a fab leans into the cycle: TSMC guided 2025 capex to $38-42B. That capex buys ever-pricier wafers (~$9,500 at N7 vs ~$16,000 at N5), while cost per transistor — the metric Moore's Law once improved automatically — gained only ~15% at N3, best case.

Finally, utilization (loadings): fabs are fixed-cost machines, so margins swing violently with how full they run. TSMC's N7 line went from 100% loaded to under 60% in the 2023 downturn — operating leverage in reverse.
sections.body_zh:
产业链自己会发布一组仪表，每一只表都给不同的层级定价。

单 GPU HBM 含量——每颗加速器上驮着多少 GB 堆叠内存——是存储板块的需求乘数。HBM 已占 H100 制造成本的 50% 以上，到 Blackwell 更超过 60%：英伟达每长大一分，SK 海力士的收入线就被机械地拖着走一分。

CoWoS 产能是出货量预报器。每一颗大型 AI GPU 都必须经过台积电的先进封装工序，所以它的月度晶圆产能——2025 年约 75,000 片/月，约为 2024 年的两倍——不管「需求」多大，都直接封死出货上限。看到任何 GPU 出货量预测，先去核 CoWoS 的算术。

WFE（晶圆制造设备）支出是设备层的收入池；而资本开支强度（capex ÷ 营收）告诉你一家厂对周期押注多深：台积电给出的 2025 年资本开支指引是 380–420 亿美元。这些钱买来的晶圆越来越贵（N7 约 9,500 美元/片，N5 约 16,000 美元），而每晶体管成本——摩尔定律时代「自动改善」的那个指标——在 N3 上最好情形也只改善约 15%。

最后是稼动率（loadings）：晶圆厂是固定成本机器，利润率随「装多满」剧烈摆动。2023 年下行周期里，台积电 N7 产线从 100% 满载跌到不足 60%——经营杠杆倒着打。

sections.heading_en: Demand-side dials: rental $/GPU-hr, revenue per MW, RPO and goodput
sections.heading_zh: 需求侧仪表盘：GPU 时租、每兆瓦收入、RPO 与 goodput
sections.body_en:
If CoWoS is the supply gauge, the rental price of compute is the demand gauge. $/GPU-hr is the closest thing AI has to a spot price. Read it against cost: an H100 runs ~$1.53/hr all-in (including debt), so a $2.00+ rental prints healthy margin, and the price's direction is a real-time shortage signal — 1-year H100 rentals rose from $1.70 to $2.35/hr (+~40%) between October 2025 and March 2026, refuting the 'old chips must collapse' consensus.

Revenue per megawatt converts power into money: an AI cloud generates roughly $10-13M per MW per year, which is why 'speed to power' is a moat — energizing 200MW six months early carries an NPV of ~$400-500M.

RPO (remaining performance obligations) is contracted-but-unrecognized revenue on cloud balance sheets — the cleanest forward-demand series in public filings. Top GPU clouds booked ~$400B of it in roughly a year; when RPO growth stalls, believe it before the keynotes.

And inside the datacenter, goodput — the share of throughput that is useful work after crashes, stragglers and restarts — separates good clouds from cheap ones. A large pretrain can lose 6% of its budget to failures on a well-run cluster and 21% on a badly run one: a 15-point tax invisible in the hourly price.
sections.body_zh:
如果说 CoWoS 是供给表，算力租金就是需求表。$/GPU-hr 是 AI 世界里最接近「现货价」的东西。读它要对着成本读：一张 H100 的全口径成本约 1.53 美元/小时（含债务成本），租到 2 美元以上就是实打实的利润；而价格的方向本身就是实时的短缺信号——2025 年 10 月到 2026 年 3 月，一年期 H100 租金从 1.70 涨到 2.35 美元/小时（+40%），直接打脸「旧芯片必然跌价」的共识。

每兆瓦收入把电变成钱：AI 云每 MW 每年大约产生 1,000–1,300 万美元收入。这就是「拿电速度即护城河」的原因——200MW 提前半年通电，其净现值约合 4–5 亿美元。

RPO（剩余履约义务）是云厂商资产负债表上「已签约、未确认」的收入——公开披露里最干净的前瞻需求序列。头部 GPU 云约一年内签下了 4,000 亿美元量级的 RPO；哪天 RPO 增速熄火，先信它，再信发布会。

而在数据中心内部，goodput——扣掉宕机、掉队节点和重启之后真正有用的产出占比——把好云和便宜云区分开：同一场大型预训练，管得好的集群损失 6% 预算，管得差的损失 21%。这 15 个百分点的税，在时租价格表上完全看不见。

sections.heading_en: Where the numbers live — and the four traps that eat analysts
sections.heading_zh: 数字都住在哪里——以及吃掉分析师的四个坑
sections.body_en:
Sources, in order of trustworthiness: (1) company IR — earnings calls, 10-K/6-K segment tables, capex guidance; TSMC even publishes monthly revenue. (2) Standards bodies (JEDEC) for what a spec actually guarantees, versus vendor overshoot. (3) Specialist trackers — TrendForce for memory pricing and CoWoS capacity, SemiAnalysis for delivered-performance benchmarks and TCO models. (4) keynotes and press releases — marketing until a number reappears in category 1.

Four traps. First, peak-FLOPS worship: spec sheets quote sparsity-doubled numbers almost no workload reaches, and cross-format comparisons flatter the newer chip — GB300 is '100x H100' only if H100 runs FP8 while GB300 runs FP4; same-format it is 65x. Always ask: which format, dense or sparse, what MFU?

Second, ignoring software maturity: MI355X throughput on DeepSeek V4 improved >100x in 26 days of kernel work. Day-0 benchmarks of new hardware are obituaries written before birth; the CUDA stack's compounding gains are a moat no spec sheet shows.

Third, announcement ≠ deployment: Microsoft walked away from >2GW of non-binding LOIs in 2025. Count binding contracts and RPO, not groundbreakings.

Fourth, know your index construction: a cap-weighted 'AI basket' is mostly Nvidia, TSMC and Broadcom — an incumbents bet — while an equal-weighted one quietly triples exposure to small, single-customer suppliers with far higher cycle beta. Same theme, different trade.
sections.body_zh:
数据来源，按可信度排序：（1）公司 IR——业绩会、10-K/6-K 分部报表、资本开支指引；台积电甚至逐月公布营收。（2）标准组织（JEDEC）——看一个规格真正承诺了什么，对照厂商的超规吹法。（3）专业追踪机构——存储价格与 CoWoS 产能看 TrendForce，实测性能与 TCO 模型看 SemiAnalysis。（4）发布会和新闻稿——一律当营销处理，直到某个数字在第 1 类来源里再次出现。

四个坑。第一，峰值 FLOPS 崇拜：规格表爱写「含稀疏」的翻倍数字（几乎没有负载能吃到），跨格式对比又天然美化新芯片——GB300「是 H100 的 100 倍」的前提是 H100 跑 FP8、GB300 跑 FP4；同格式下是 65 倍。永远追问三件事：什么格式？dense 还是 sparse？MFU 多少？

第二，无视软件成熟度：MI355X 跑 DeepSeek V4 的吞吐在 26 天的 kernel 优化里提升超 100 倍。新硬件的首日评测是出生前写好的讣告——而 CUDA 栈的复利式改进，是任何规格表都不会展示的护城河。

第三，官宣 ≠ 落地：微软 2025 年放弃了超过 2GW 的非约束性意向书。要数有约束力的合同和 RPO，别数奠基仪式。

第四，看清指数构造：市值加权的「AI 篮子」基本就是英伟达+台积电+博通——押的是在位者；等权重的篮子则悄悄把小市值、单一客户供应商的敞口放大三倍，周期 beta 高得多。同一个主题，完全不同的交易。

sections.heading_en: The glossary: the whole series in forty-three lines
sections.heading_zh: 术语表：四十三行装下整个系列
sections.body_en:
One line each, in supply-chain order.

**Process node (N5/N3/N2/A16)** — marketing labels for transistor generations; the numbers measure nothing physical anymore.
**Foundry / fabless / IDM** — who owns the fab: contract maker (TSMC), designer-only (Nvidia), or both-in-one (Intel).
**Yield / D0** — share of dies that work; D0 is defects per cm², the number that decides it.
**Tape-out / mask set** — freezing a design into photomasks; ~$40M per set at 3nm.
**EUV / High-NA EUV** — ASML-monopoly lithography (~$225M/tool; High-NA ~$400M) printing the finest layers.
**Multi-patterning** — splitting one fine layer across several cheaper exposures.
**WFE** — wafer-fab equipment: the toolset (litho, etch, deposition, metrology) every fab must buy.
**Utilization / loadings** — how full a fab runs; margins swing violently with it.
**FinFET / GAA** — transistor architectures; GAA wraps the gate fully around the channel to keep scaling alive.
**Backside power delivery** — power wiring moved beneath the transistors, freeing the top for signals.
**Cost per transistor** — the metric Moore's Law used to improve automatically; now nearly stalled.
**HBM / TSV** — DRAM dies stacked 8-16 high, wired by through-silicon vias; the memory that feeds GPUs at 3x+ DDR5 prices.
**Stack-yield compounding** — per-layer yield multiplied across 12 layers; why HBM supply lags.
**Memory wall** — compute outgrew memory bandwidth; most inference is bound by bytes, not FLOPS.
**wspm** — wafer starts per month; the fab capacity unit.
**Advanced packaging** — bump pitch below 100µm; chip-to-chip wiring as leading-edge tech.
**CoWoS / interposer** — TSMC's 2.5D packaging seating GPU and HBM on a silicon carrier; gates AI GPU output.
**Chiplet / KGD** — splitting one big die into tested smaller ones ('known good die') to beat defect math.
**Hybrid bonding** — direct copper-to-copper stacking at micron pitches; the 3D endgame.
**OSAT** — outsourced assembly-and-test firms (ASE, Amkor), the packaging tier below foundries.
**SIMT / SM** — the GPU model: thousands of threads in lockstep across streaming multiprocessors.
**Tensor Core / systolic array** — dedicated matrix engines amortizing instruction overhead into hundreds of ops.
**Peak FLOPS** — the paper ceiling; often quoted 'with sparsity' at 2x what workloads reach.
**FP8 / FP4** — low-precision formats; each halving doubles peak math if accuracy holds.
**MFU** — model FLOPS utilization: delivered ÷ peak; 30-55% in real training.
**Perf/W** — tokens per watt; the ranking that matters when power is the binding constraint.
**TCO / perf-per-TCO** — all-in ownership cost, and the industry's true purchasing yardstick.
**Scale-up vs scale-out** — the rack-level all-to-all fabric (NVLink, ~9x+ the cluster network, roughly an order of magnitude) vs the cluster network.
**World size** — accelerators per scale-up domain (NVL72 = 72); decides which models serve cheaply.
**Transceiver / DSP** — pluggable optics; the biggest cost and power line in cluster networking.
**CPO** — co-packaged optics: optics moved onto the switch package, deleting the DSP and ~2/3 of link power.
**OCS** — optical circuit switching with mirrors (~108W vs ~3,000W electronic); Google's pod glue.
**PUE / WUE** — facility power (or water) ÷ IT power; hyperscalers ~1.1 vs industry ~1.6.
**BTM** — behind-the-meter: building your own generation instead of queueing years for grid power.
**$/GPU-hr / neocloud** — compute's spot price, and the GPU-only clouds (CoreWeave) that set it.
**RPO** — contracted-but-unrecognized revenue; the cleanest forward-demand gauge in filings.
**Goodput** — useful work as a share of raw throughput, after failures and restarts.
**$/Mtok** — dollars per million tokens; the end-product unit cost the whole chain works to cut.
**Prefill vs decode** — inference's two phases: compute-bound prompt reading vs bandwidth-bound token writing.
**KV cache** — stored attention state growing with context length; long-context memory hog.
**MoE** — mixture-of-experts: only a slice of the model computes per token, trading FLOPS for memory and interconnect.
**FDPR / Entity List** — US export-control levers: reach foreign-made goods via US tech content; blacklist named firms.
**Die bank** — stockpiled dies (Huawei banked 2.9M+ from TSMC) that keep a sanctioned firm shipping for years.
sections.body_zh:
每词一行，按产业链顺序排列。

**制程节点（N5/N3/N2/A16）**——晶体管世代的营销代号；数字早已不对应任何物理尺寸。
**Foundry / fabless / IDM**——谁拥有晶圆厂：代工厂（台积电）、只设计不制造（英伟达）、设计制造一体（英特尔）。
**良率 / D0**——晶圆上能用的裸片占比；D0 即每平方厘米缺陷数，是良率的决定量。
**流片 / 掩模套组**——把设计冻结进光掩模；3nm 一套约 4,000 万美元。
**EUV / High-NA EUV**——ASML 垄断的光刻（约 2.25 亿美元/台；High-NA 约 4 亿），负责最精细的层。
**多重曝光**——把一层精细图形拆成多次更便宜的曝光。
**WFE**——晶圆制造设备：光刻、刻蚀、沉积、量测等所有晶圆厂必买的工具。
**稼动率（loadings）**——晶圆厂装多满；固定成本让利润率随之剧烈摆动。
**FinFET / GAA**——晶体管架构；GAA 让栅极全包沟道，把微缩续命。
**背面供电**——电源布线挪到晶体管下方，把正面全部让给信号。
**每晶体管成本**——摩尔定律时代「自动改善」的指标；如今近乎停滞。
**HBM / TSV**——8–16 层 DRAM 裸片经硅通孔堆叠；喂 GPU 的内存，单价是 DDR5 的 3 倍以上。
**堆叠良率累乘**——单层良率在 12 层上连乘；HBM 供给追不上需求的原因。
**存储墙**——算力增速远超内存带宽；多数推理卡在字节而非 FLOPS。
**wspm**——每月晶圆投片数，晶圆厂的产能单位。
**先进封装**——凸点间距小于 100µm；芯片间布线本身成了尖端技术。
**CoWoS / 中介层**——台积电 2.5D 封装，把 GPU 和 HBM 放上同一块硅载板；卡死 AI GPU 产出。
**Chiplet / KGD**——把大芯片拆成先测试后组装的小裸片（「已知良品裸片」），赢下缺陷数学。
**混合键合**——微米级间距的铜对铜直接堆叠；3D 封装的终局。
**OSAT**——委外封测厂（日月光、Amkor），代工厂之下的封装层。
**SIMT / SM**——GPU 的执行模型：数千线程在流式多处理器上齐步走。
**Tensor Core / 脉动阵列**——专用矩阵引擎，把指令开销摊薄进几百次运算。
**峰值 FLOPS**——纸面天花板；常按「含稀疏」翻倍报，几乎没有负载吃得到。
**FP8 / FP4**——低精度格式；每砍半一次，峰值算力翻倍——前提是精度守得住。
**MFU**——模型算力利用率：实际 ÷ 峰值；真实训练 30–55%。
**Perf/W**——每瓦 token 数；电力成为硬约束后真正的排位赛。
**TCO / perf-per-TCO**——全口径拥有成本，及行业真正的采购标尺。
**Scale-up 与 scale-out**——机柜级全互连织物（NVLink，约为集群网络的 9 倍以上，约一个数量级）对集群网络。
**World size**——单个 scale-up 域里的加速器数量（NVL72 = 72）；决定哪些模型能便宜地服务。
**光模块 / DSP**——可插拔光收发器；集群网络里最大的成本与功耗项。
**CPO**——共封装光学：把光学搬进交换芯片封装、删掉 DSP，省约 2/3 链路功耗。
**OCS**——用镜子做光路交换（约 108W 对电交换约 3,000W）；谷歌 TPU pod 的粘合剂。
**PUE / WUE**——设施总功耗（或用水）÷ IT 功耗；超大规模厂约 1.1，行业均值约 1.6。
**BTM**——表后供电：自建电源，不排几年队等电网。
**$/GPU-hr / neocloud**——算力现货价，以及为它定价的纯 GPU 云（CoreWeave）。
**RPO**——已签约未确认收入；公开披露里最干净的前瞻需求表。
**Goodput**——扣除故障与重启后，真正有用的产出占总吞吐之比。
**$/Mtok**——每百万 token 成本；整条链条拼命压低的终端单位成本。
**Prefill 与 decode**——推理两阶段：算力吃紧的读题 vs 带宽吃紧的写答案。
**KV cache**——随上下文长度膨胀的注意力状态缓存；长上下文的内存大户。
**MoE**——专家混合：每个 token 只激活模型一小片，用内存和互连换 FLOPS。
**FDPR / 实体清单**——美国出口管制的两把手术刀：凭美国技术含量管到外国造的货；点名拉黑公司。
**Die bank（裸片银行）**——囤积的裸片库存（华为从台积电囤了 290 万+），让被制裁公司还能出货数年。

key_numbers.metric_en: GB200 NVL72 total cost of ownership vs an equivalent H100 fleet
key_numbers.metric_zh: GB200 NVL72 对等量 H100 机队的总拥有成本
key_numbers.value: ~1.6x — so it must be ≥1.6x faster to win on perf-per-TCO
key_numbers.source_name: SemiAnalysis — H100 vs GB200 NVL72 Training Benchmarks - Power, TCO, and Reliability Analysis

key_numbers.metric_en: H100 training MFU gain from software alone (12 months, no hardware change)
key_numbers.metric_zh: H100 训练 MFU 纯软件提升（12 个月，硬件不变）

images.source_name: Wikimedia Commons (CC BY 2.0, photo by Rob Bulmahn)
images.caption_en: A patterned silicon wafer — the industry's unit of account. Wafer starts per month, cost per wafer, yield and utilization all price this one object; a leading-edge wafer now costs ~$16,000 at N5 vs ~$9,500 at N7.
images.caption_zh: 一片已光刻的硅晶圆——整个行业的记账单位。每月投片数、每片成本、良率、稼动率，全都在给这一件东西定价；先进制程晶圆 N5 已约 16,000 美元/片（N7 约 9,500 美元）。

images.caption_en: SK hynix HBM3E, lidded and bare. 'HBM content per GPU' — the gigabytes of this stacked DRAM riding on each accelerator — is now the single biggest line in an AI GPU's bill of materials (~50-60% of manufacturing cost).
images.caption_zh: SK 海力士 HBM3E（带盖与裸片）。「单 GPU HBM 含量」——每颗加速器驮着多少 GB 这种堆叠 DRAM——如今是 AI GPU 物料清单里最大的一项（约占制造成本 50–60%）。

own_fig.concept_en: The metric map: one gauge per layer of the AI supply chain, and where to read it
own_fig.concept_zh: 指标地图：AI 产业链每一层各配一只表，以及去哪里读数
own_fig.spec: Vertical flow diagram with 8 stacked layer-boxes (top = upstream) and 3 columns per row: [Layer] | [The metric that prices it] | [Where to read it]. Rows: (1) Equipment — 'WFE spend' — ASML/AMAT/Lam/TEL earnings calls; (2) Foundry — 'cost per wafer, utilization, capex intensity' — TSMC IR (monthly revenue; 2025 capex guide $38-42B, pr.tsmc.com/english/news/3201); (3) Memory — 'HBM content per GPU, $/GB' — memory-maker calls + TrendForce; (4) Packaging — 'CoWoS wafers/month' — TrendForce (75k wpm 2025, ~2x 2024); (5) Accelerator — 'perf per TCO, MFU, perf/W' — vendor specs cross-checked with independent benchmarks (SemiAnalysis: GB200 NVL72 TCO ~1.6x H100; H100 BF16 MFU 34→54% software-only); (6) Networking — 'cost & watts per transceiver, ports per GPU' — Broadcom/Arista calls, cluster BOM models; (7) Datacenter/Power — '$/MW, PUE, revenue/MW ~$10-13M/yr' — utility filings + hyperscaler sustainability reports; (8) Cloud & Model — '$/GPU-hr and $/Mtok' — neocloud rate cards and API price sheets (1-yr H100 $1.70→$2.35/hr Oct-25→Mar-26; GB300 NVL72 $0.156/Mtok on DeepSeek V4 — SemiAnalysis). A single bold arrow runs down the right side labeled 'each layer's output metric = the next layer's input cost'. Bottom banner lists the four traps as icons: peak-FLOPS worship / software maturity / announcement≠deployment / index construction.

why_en: Metrics are where theses live or die. The investor who prices peak FLOPS buys the keynote; the one who prices perf-per-TCO, MFU and $/Mtok buys the cash flow. This module hands you the full instrument panel — supply gauges (CoWoS wafers, HBM content, WFE, capex intensity, utilization) and demand gauges ($/GPU-hr, revenue per MW, RPO, goodput) — plus the map of where each number is published and the four traps that most reliably destroy AI hardware analysis. With the forty-three-term glossary, every sector brief in this series becomes readable at full speed, and every vendor claim becomes checkable arithmetic.
why_zh: 投资论点的生死都在指标上。给峰值 FLOPS 定价的人买到的是发布会，给 perf-per-TCO、MFU 和 $/Mtok 定价的人买到的才是现金流。本模块交付整块仪表盘——供给侧（CoWoS 投片、HBM 含量、WFE、资本开支强度、稼动率）与需求侧（GPU 时租、每兆瓦收入、RPO、goodput）——外加每个数字的出处地图，和最常摧毁 AI 硬件分析的四个坑。配上四十三词术语表，本系列每一篇行业简报都能全速阅读，每一句厂商话术都能变成可验算的算术。
